# machinen

> A VM with a single goal: hand off between hosts. Freeze a Linux microVM on one machine, thaw it on another; the program picks up exactly where it left off — like waking a laptop from sleep, except on a different computer.

A native arm64 microVM runtime. Node.js is the first-class target; Python, bash, and anything else that boots in a Linux VM works too. Snapshot, restore, and fork live processes across hosts with their heap, file descriptors, and timers intact.

The runtime source is not yet published. The companion website source is at https://github.com/redwoodjs/machinen.dev.

## Pages

- [Landing](https://machinen.dev/index.md): One-line pitch and project status.

## Reference

- [README](https://github.com/redwoodjs/machinen.dev/blob/main/README.md): Install, quickstart (bake / boot / handoff), fork, and runtime API.
- [Repository](https://github.com/redwoodjs/machinen.dev): Marketing site source. Runtime source is not yet public.
